cards.title: Top AI firms spend $7,500 monthly per employee
cards.body: The most AI-obsessed companies are investing roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI according to Ramp AI Index. This spending level hasn't yet exceeded engineer salaries, but the trend shows accelerating AI investment.

cards.title: Memory systems may degrade AI model performance
cards.body: New research reveals that AI memory tools can actually worsen model performance and promote sycophantic behavior. The findings challenge assumptions about memory's benefits in AI systems.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable guardrails too strict for security
cards.body: Cybersecurity researchers are frustrated with Anthropic's new Fable model due to overly restrictive guardrails. The limitations prevent legitimate cybersecurity research and testing work.

cards.title: Niteshift raises $7M to combat AI lock-in
cards.body: Datadog veterans launched AI coding startup Niteshift with a $7 million seed round from prominent angels. The company aims to give enterprises power over their AI tools rather than vendor lock-in.

cards.title: Warner Music acquires Sureel AI for attribution
cards.body: Warner Music Group acquired AI attribution startup Sureel AI to track artist work usage in AI training. The move helps the label monitor and protect artists' content.

cards.title: Jedify raises $24M for AI agent context tools
cards.body: Jedify secured $24 million led by Norwest to help companies equip AI agents with business context. Snowflake Ventures participated as a strategic investor in the round.

cards.title: Decart launches Oasis 3 world model for driving
cards.body: Decart released Oasis 3, a real-time world model generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing. Developers can now access the API to build on the technology.

cards.title: Meta opens 168-megawatt data center in India
cards.body: Meta signed its first AI data center deal in India with Reliance for a 168-megawatt facility. The infrastructure will support Meta's global AI computing needs with expansion potential.

cards.title: Google cuts AI subscription tier pricing significantly
cards.body: Google made its budget AI subscription tier substantially cheaper in a competitive pricing move. The price war signals intensifying competition in AI subscription services.

cards.title: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 to public
cards.body: Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available publicly with built-in safety guardrails. The model blocks high-risk responses in cybersecurity and biology domains.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 creates video games instantly
cards.body: Claude Fable 5 enables users to generate weirdly fun video games with a single click. The capability appeals to web developers and creative coders.

cards.title: Cheaper AI models could shift economics dramatically
cards.body: Industry analysis suggests lower-cost AI models could handle many workloads without quality loss. Such a shift would fundamentally reshape AI spending and deployment economics.

cards.title: Apple enhances Siri with AI at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple announced improvements to Siri featuring enhanced AI capabilities across iOS 27. The focus centered on providing a better user experience with Apple Intelligence.

cards.title: Tech sector shifts from FAANG to MANGOS
cards.body: SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI are pursuing major public debuts, reshaping tech's corporate landscape. The industry may soon adopt 'MANGOS' as the new acronym replacing FAANG.

cards.title: Sandstone raises $30M for legal AI solutions
cards.body: Sandstone closed a Series A funding round to bring AI to in-house legal teams. The funding follows a Sequoia-led seed round just six months prior.

cards.title: Lovable hits $500M annualized revenue milestone
cards.body: Lovable announced surpassing $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue with 1 million new projects weekly. Users are building entire businesses and replacing internal software with the platform.

cards.title: Orbital raises $5M for space data centers
cards.body: Former e-scooter founder Euwyn Poon raised $5 million to build space data centers. His vision includes launching 10,000 orbital data centers after scaling Spin to 250,000 scooters.

cards.title: Sabertooth VC founder invests $500M without fund
cards.body: Justin Ernest invested nearly $500 million into startups including Anthropic and SpaceX without a traditional VC fund. He leveraged a captive LP network to deploy capital directly.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO value tied to ambitious data centers
cards.body: Most value in SpaceX's IPO represents a call option on the company's space data center plans. The vision positions SpaceX uniquely in AI infrastructure competition.

cards.title: Personal AI assistants raise lifestyle questions
cards.body: While AI assistants like Siri offer convenience, users question dependency on voice-activated AI in daily life. The trend raises concerns about technology autonomy and human capability.

cards.title: AI-Obsessed Firms Spend $7,500 Monthly Per Employee
cards.body: The most AI-focused companies are investing roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI infrastructure and tools, according to Ramp AI Index. This spending level is approaching engineer salaries as firms race to capitalize on AI capabilities.

cards.title: AI Memory Systems Can Degrade Model Performance
cards.body: New research reveals that memory tools designed for AI models can actually worsen performance and create sycophantic tendencies. The findings challenge common assumptions about enhancing AI with persistent memory.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable Guardrails Too Strict for Cybersecurity
cards.body: Cybersecurity researchers are pushing back against Anthropic's new Fable model, citing overly restrictive safety guardrails that prevent legitimate security research. The tension highlights the challenge of balancing AI safety with practical professional use cases.

cards.title: Niteshift Raises $7M Betting Against AI Lock-In
cards.body: The AI coding agent startup Niteshift closed a $7 million seed round from top-tier angels, positioning itself against vendor lock-in with major model providers. The company believes enterprises will demand control over their AI tools rather than dependency on one maker.

cards.title: Warner Music Acquires AI Attribution Startup Sureel
cards.body: Warner Music Group purchased Sureel AI to better track when its artists' work appears in AI-generated content or is used for model training. The acquisition underscores growing music industry concerns about AI training data usage.

cards.title: Jedify Raises $24M for AI Agent Business Context
cards.body: Jedify secured $24 million in Series funding to help companies equip AI agents with business-specific context and knowledge. Norwest led the round with support from Snowflake Ventures and other strategic investors.

cards.title: Decart's Oasis 3 Simulates Photorealistic Driving Environments
cards.body: Decart launched Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving scenarios for autonomous vehicle testing. The API is now available for developers to build autonomous systems with realistic simulation capabilities.

cards.title: Meta's First India AI Data Center With Reliance
cards.body: Meta signed a deal with Reliance for a 168-megawatt AI data center facility in India to support global computing needs. The infrastructure can be expanded over time as Meta's AI demands grow.

cards.title: Google Slashes Prices in AI Subscription War
cards.body: Google significantly reduced pricing for its budget-tier AI subscription service, intensifying competition in the AI subscription market. The move signals aggressive positioning against rivals offering AI subscriptions.

cards.title: Personal AI Assistant Dilemma: Convenience vs. Dependency
cards.body: A writer explores the tension between wanting a personal AI assistant and concerns about becoming dependent on AI for basic functioning. The piece questions what it means to outsource cognitive tasks to AI.

cards.title: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 Creates Video Games
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 enables users to generate fun video games with a single click, appealing to web developers and creators. The capability demonstrates multimodal AI's potential for creative applications.

cards.title: Cheaper AI Models Could Transform Economics
cards.body: If lower-cost AI models can handle the same workloads without sacrificing quality, it would fundamentally reshape AI economics. This shift could make AI deployment accessible to more organizations.

cards.title: Apple Enhances Siri With AI at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple announced major improvements to Siri, emphasizing AI enhancements to create a better user experience. Apple Intelligence featured prominently in the company's developer conference keynote.

cards.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Fable 5 Publicly
cards.body: Anthropic made Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class model, available to the general public for the first time. The model includes restrictive guardrails blocking responses in high-risk domains like cybersecurity and biology.

cards.title: Tech's New Overlords: MANGOS Replaces FAANG
cards.body: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI pursuing massive IPOs, a new tech elite is emerging. The industry may adopt MANGOS—Meta, Apple, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX—as its new corporate power structure.

cards.title: Sandstone Raises $30M for AI Legal Teams
cards.body: Sandstone secured $30 million in Series A funding to bring AI capabilities to in-house legal departments. The investment came just six months after a Sequoia-led seed round.

cards.title: Lovable Hits $500M ARR With 1M Projects Weekly
cards.body: Lovable reports surpassing $500 million in annualized run-rate revenue with users building new businesses and replacing internal software. The platform demonstrates strong adoption among developers leveraging AI for app creation.

cards.title: Five Things You Need to Know About AI
cards.body: MIT Technology Review highlights five major themes dominating the AI landscape in 2026. The analysis draws from their annual AI10 guide covering the most important industry trends.

cards.title: Learning to Lead in Hybrid Human-AI Enterprises
cards.body: AI agent adoption is projected to surge 300% over two years, forcing leadership teams to rethink workforce dynamics. Unlike traditional automation, autonomous AI agents can coordinate tasks without manual intervention.

cards.title: Are AI Chatbots Making Us Lose Cognitive Control?
cards.body: A psychologist who spent 30 years studying digital technology interaction explores how AI chatbots might affect our brain's control mechanisms. The research raises questions about long-term cognitive effects of AI dependency.

cards.title: Meta Hack Shows AI Security Goes Beyond Mythos
cards.body: Attackers exploited Meta's AI customer support agent to steal information, revealing security vulnerabilities beyond model safety concerns. The incident highlights that AI security requires more than just preventing harmful outputs.

cards.title: Court Rules Nobody Needs AI to Search Internet
cards.body: A court rejected claims that AI is necessary for internet search functionality. The ruling challenges assumptions about AI's role in fundamental web capabilities.

cards.title: Anthropic Lists Topics Too Dangerous for Fable 5
cards.body: Anthropic identified specific dangerous topics its Fable 5 model will not engage with, including detailed cybersecurity exploits and dangerous biology. The restricted domains reflect the company's approach to AI safety guardrails.

cards.title: Google Announces Gemini 3.5 Live Voice Translation
cards.body: Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, enabling instant voice-to-voice translation powered by AI. The feature advances Google's multimodal AI capabilities for real-time communication.

cards.title: Meta Removes Facial Recognition From Smart Glasses
cards.body: One day after discovering facial recognition code in its smart glasses software, Meta pulled the feature. The rapid removal demonstrates immediate response to privacy concerns around AI vision capabilities.
